item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-011","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":false,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The answer correctly reframes 'put docs into prompt' as only the interface/context-injection surface, and identifies retrieval/recall as the role of the vector store. It covers the actual controlling factors: corpus size, semantic matching quality, update/indexing needs, permissions/source handling, cost and latency. It also gives concrete consequences of cutting the vector store in non-trivial cases: inability to fit all docs, weaker semantic matching, slower/less precise location of relevant passages, harder updates/permissions/incremental indexing, and higher prompt cost/latency. It does not simply agree to cut the vector database to save money."}
